steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: This is a main-purpose question. We must identify why the narrator wrote this short passage, not just what it mentions.

steps.title: Trace the narrator's logic
steps.content: The narrator says there is "just a chance that I shall make it" (the team), then justifies that hope by listing her physical traits (little, quick, wiry, tough) and her tactical edge (dodging under players' feet to grab the ball). The whole text exists to support that claim.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'compare basketball with other sports': no other sport is mentioned anywhere in the passage, so there is no comparison being made. ✗
  • Choice B — 'provide details of how to play basketball': the narrator describes her own strengths, not rules, strategies, or instructions for playing the sport. ✗
  • Choice C — 'state how players will be chosen for the basketball team': the passage never describes the selection process, tryouts, or criteria coaches will use; it only describes the narrator's self-assessment. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
